feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: EU's cloud and AI development act gets mixed reception
entry.content: The European Commission's goal of tripling the EU’s data centre market within five to seven years would not be possible without intrusive market engineering, and CADA does exactly that.

entry.title: The only way to get good at AI is to fail at it, says AWS’s chief marketing officer Julia White
entry.content: "AI is a wonderful thought partner — but it's not a tastemaker," says AWS marketing chief Julie White in an interview with Euronews Next.

entry.title: Kazakhstan's President in Brussels: Focus on energy, transportation routes, AI, and more
entry.content: Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev is visiting Brussels. His goal: to deepen cooperation between the EU and Kazakhstan. The 73-year-old previously outlined what this might look like in an op-ed for Euronews. We have summarized the key points.

entry.title: Spain's Supreme Court jails former socialist minister for 24 years in COVID corruption case
entry.content: A major blow to Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, Spain's Supreme Court has sentenced former socialist minister and close Sánchez ally José Luis Ábalos to 24 years in prison in a corruption case linked to COVID-19 mask contracts.

entry.title: Assouline launches first-ever travel series kiosk at Doha's Hamad International Airport
entry.content: Travellers passing through Doha will be able to buy books, candles, totes and other gifts from the luxury publisher.

entry.title: Sesame Street scandal: Elmo’s World Cup support reveals Republican ‘hypocrisy’
entry.content: A wholesome clip of Sesame Street's Elmo saying he is supporting Team USA during the FIFA World Cup has gone viral, and the House Republicans could not resist posting a reply... It has not gone down well.

entry.title: Octola III: Embrace Arctic ultra-luxury at this invitation-only retreat in Finnish Lapland
entry.content: Opening in December, this private lodge accommodates just six guests and access is granted exclusively through an invitation-only waiting list.

entry.title: 10 years of Brexit: Which campaign claims have come true?
entry.content: A decade on from the referendum that led to the UK leaving the European Union, Euronews' fact-checking team looks at the campaign promises that were made on both the Leave and Remain sides to see which ones ended up happening.

entry.title: Moldova's EU accession and Transnistria settlement are 'parallel processes', minister says
entry.content: Moldovan Foreign Minister Mihai Popșoi said the country's EU accession process and the Transnistria conflict are "parallel" tracks to prevent Vladimir Putin from gaining "leverage". Moldova is working "tirelessly" to ensure all Moldovans benefit from accession, said the minister.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 22nd, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 22nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
entry.content: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

entry.title: The party is over: Why France is looking to tighten its grip on free parties
entry.content: On Monday, the French National Assembly will start reviewing a new governmental bill aiming to crack down on unauthorised outdoor gatherings, or 'free parties'.

entry.title: Cape Verde produce another World Cup shock to keep remarkable run alive
entry.content: Cape Verde supporters in the United States celebrated after their team scored its first-ever World Cup goals and earned a 2-2 draw against Uruguay.

entry.title: Daft Punk’s ‘One More Time’ becomes French team’s official 2026 World Cup goal song
entry.content: Les Bleus’ next World Cup game is set for tonight against Iraq, and their chosen soundtrack is already delighting music fans...

entry.title: China announces sanctions on 10 US companies as trade tensions flare
entry.content: The sanctions come a month after US President Donald Trump visited Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping aimed at stabilising relations.

entry.title: Venice to Istanbul: Explore more of the Balkans on the Golden Eagle Danube Express
entry.content: Golden Eagle Luxury Trains has refreshed its ‘Balkan Explorer’ rail route with an overnight stay in Belgrade, new Croatian stops and extended time in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

entry.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row
entry.content: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

entry.title: Australian police seize nearly 3 tonnes of cocaine in what has become the largest bust on record
entry.content: The bust of nearly 3 tonnes is the country’s largest ever and is believed to hold a street value of just shy of €500 million.

entry.title: BABELL turns Porto into a 'book city' with internationally renowned authors
entry.content: Two Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners and writers will make Porto a literary capital from 24 to 29 June at the BABELL Festival, to bring readers closer to bookshops.

entry.title: Can the EU fix the gender pay gap? Take our poll.
entry.content: The gender pay gap a persistent problem in Europe, where men earn, on average 12.7 percent more per hour. The EU has just implemented its new Pay Transparency Directive, to pull the curtain back on salary secrecy and empower employees.

entry.title: Newsletter: A Commissioner's secret trip to Israel
entry.content: In this newsletter: A high-ranking EU official visits Jerusalem on Monday, exposing a vacuum of information about the trip; UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to lay-out how he will vacate Number 10 Downing Street; plus, the EU-Moldova summit gets underway in the Belgian capital.

entry.title: 'Toy Story 5' breaks record and scores biggest box office debut of 2026
entry.content: Pixar’s Toy Story franchise has proven its enduring appeal, with the fifth instalment shattering box office records and securing the biggest opening weekend of 2026.

entry.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup
entry.content: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: Europe Today: Starmer set to step down as US-Iran talks and Moldova summit unfold
entry.content: US-Iran talks continued after a temporary pause threatened the fragile agreement, as Brussels hosted the second EU-Moldova summit focused on Chişinău's membership bid. Meanwhile, pressure grew on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer as tensions between Poland and Ukraine continued to escalate.

entry.title: How does the EU ensure pay transparency? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot
entry.content: The EU is rewriting compensation rules. Member states had until 7 June to implement the Pay Transparency Directive, which addresses pay transparency and gender pay gap. But what does it change in practice? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot.

entry.title: Supergirl, Frida Kahlo and dragons: What to see, do or hear this week in Europe
entry.content: A major new Frida Kahlo exhibition arrives in London, Supergirl blasts onto the big screen, and House of the Dragon returns for a third season. Here's what to look out for this week.

entry.title: Mandarin Oriental is set to return to this southeast Asian capital this winter
entry.content: Luxury hospitality brand will return to Manila after a 14-year absence, bringing with it suites with skyline views and a wellness floor inspired by Filipino traditions.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 22nd, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 22nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Kazakhstan and the European Union: Building the next chapter of a strategic partnership
entry.content: The partnership between Kazakhstan and the EU is more important today than ever before, writes President of the Republic of Kazakhstan Kassym-Jomart Tokayev in an opinion article for Euronews, identifying three key strategic objectives to build the next chapter of a strategic partnership.

entry.title: Major explosion at Qatar's largest energy site injures dozens as 18 remain unaccounted for
entry.content: Qatar’s Interior Ministry says search and rescue operations are continuing to locate close to two dozen people who remain missing following the large explosion at the country’s main energy site.

entry.title: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
entry.content: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

entry.title: Spain clinch their first win of the tournament in 4-0 drubbing of Saudi Arabia
entry.content: 18-year-old Spain star Lamine Yamal took just 10 minutes in his first World Cup start to score his first ever goal on the sport's biggest stage.

entry.title: James Burrows, director of beloved sitcom "Friends", dies aged 85
entry.content: James Burrows won 11 Emmy awards during his career and worked as director, producer and writer in more than 1,000 episodes of hit TV programmes.

entry.title: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
entry.content: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

entry.title: Drone show in Matosinhos breaks Guinness record
entry.content: Almost 3,100 drones flew in sync in a show by Catalan Flock Drone Art Studio, which also marked the opening of the Sagrada Família’s Tower of Jesus, attended by Pope Leo XVI.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 21st, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 21st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: A Spectacular Finish to the Ulaanbaatar Grand Slam
entry.content: The Ulaanbaatar Grand Slam concluded with outstanding performances from the world’s top judoka. MURAO Sanshiro claimed a ninth Grand Slam title, LYTVYNENKO Yelyzaveta triumphed in -78kg, while PIRELLI Gennaro, ARAI Mao and KIM Minjong secured gold on a thrilling final day in Mongolia.

entry.title: Starmer reflecting on 'political realities' amid reports of imminent resignation
entry.content: The British PM has repeatedly vowed to fight on in his role but has come under increasing pressure in recent weeks.

entry.title: These Gulf airlines are offering insurance to entice passengers back to the Middle East
entry.content: Both Emirates and Etihad Airways have announced new insurance schemes which will protect travellers whose countries still list the region as a no-go zone.

entry.title: France bans alcohol at Fête de la Musique as heatwave grips Europe
entry.content: Sunday is the summer solstice, meaning the sun is at its strongest and UV levels will be high across the continent.

entry.title: Zanzibar travel guide: Best things to do, where to stay and top tips for visitors
entry.content: Tanzania's island paradise has emerged as one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing travel destinations.

entry.title: Rare medieval notebook found in Paderborn latrine
entry.content: Archaeologists have unearthed a medieval notebook in a former latrine in Paderborn. The remarkably well-preserved leather, wood and wax find may shed new light on daily life in the 13th and 14th centuries.

entry.title: Rare medieval notebook found in latrine in Paderborn
entry.content: Archaeologists have uncovered a medieval notebook in a former latrine in Paderborn. The remarkably well-preserved leather, wood and wax find may shed new light on everyday life in the 13th and 14th centuries.

entry.title: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
entry.content: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

entry.title: Poland and Ukraine's ‘honours war’ intensifies
entry.content: After Poland’s president stripped his Ukrainian counterpart of the country’s highest state honour, current and former Ukrainian officials are renouncing their own awards.

entry.title: Japan fans keep World Cup clean-up tradition alive
entry.content: Japanese fans once again cleaned the stands after Japan's 4-0 World Cup victory over Tunisia, continuing a tradition that has become a symbol of respect and sportsmanship. Supporters collected rubbish in the stadium before leaving, a gesture that has drawn admiration at every World Cup since 1998.

entry.title: Show-stopping astrotourism centre with stargazing lodge to open near Saudi Arabia’s AlUla
entry.content: Located within a Dark Sky Park, AlUla Manara’s design was inspired by the spiralling forms of galaxies and planetary rings.

entry.title: Russian-occupied Crimea suspends petrol sales amid fuel crisis
entry.content: Kyiv's targeting of Russia's energy industry has sparked a major fuel crisis in Crimea, with long queues for petrol stations and limited supply.

entry.title: US and Iranian delegations arrive in Switzerland for peace deal talks
entry.content: "We're going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue," US Vice President JD Vance told reporters.

entry.title: This cave village in France offers a natural escape from extreme heat
entry.content: Residents of the troglodyte village of Trôo say their cave homes remain naturally cool year-round, offering an energy-efficient refuge as heatwaves become more frequent across France.

entry.title: Fête de la Musique 2026: When the whole of France becomes one big music festival
entry.content: If you are out and about anywhere in France on 21 June 2026, your ears will be in for a treat as the 45th edition of the now world-famous music event "Fête de la musique" takes place. The lineup country-wide is very promising...

entry.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
entry.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

entry.title: From ruin to restoration: Byzantine Greek Monastery becomes 'Art Reliquary' for spiritual works
entry.content: The imposing, restored tower of Panachrantou Monastery has been transformed into “Art Reliquary – a gallery devoted to spiritual and contemporary creativity.”

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 21st, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 21st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Forget the Camino de Santiago. Romania’s Via Transilvanica hiking trail is wild, welcoming and quiet
entry.content: The hiking trail runs 1,600km across Romania through 400 villages in 20 different ethnic and cultural regions.

entry.title: Meet Osito, the rescue dog winning hearts at the World Cup
entry.content: A rescue dog named Osito has become an unlikely World Cup star in Mexico City, attracting crowds, selfies and social media attention wherever he goes.

entry.title: World Cup: Netherlands bounces back to lead Group F with 5-1 win against Sweden
entry.content: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each to power Netherlands to a 5-1 win over Sweden to bounce back after a disappointing draw in its opener and move atop the Group F standings.

entry.title: From Évian to Brussels: Inside a whirlwind week of high-stakes diplomacy
entry.content: In her weekly newsletter, Euronews' Chief Europe Editor, Maria Tadeo, takes readers behind the scenes of a whirlwind week, from Macron's "perfect" G7 in Évian to Costa's call with Russia, which she argues comes with the job.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 20th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 20th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Wife of Spanish PM Pedro Pedro Sánchez has passport revoked as court orders corruption trial
entry.content: The case is one of several corruption affairs that have embroiled the Socialist leader’s family and former allies, threatening to topple his minority coalition government.

entry.title: Judo: Home nation strikes gold in Ulaanbaatar
entry.content: The hosts celebrated gold on day two as Ankhzaya Lavjargal thrilled the Mongolian crowd with victory in the -73kg category. World champions Haruka Kaju and Shiho Tanaka added more gold for Japan, while Korea’s Lee Joonhwan impressed with a spectacular ippon to claim the -81kg title.

entry.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon
entry.content: It comes after Israel carried out a wave of fresh strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday morning.

entry.title: Trump doubles down on Meloni comments, saying she asked 'over and over' for photo
entry.content: Writing on Truth Social, Trump said: "She is doing poorly in Italy with her level of popularity".

entry.title: Lightning strikes injure 10 in southern Germany
entry.content: Lightning struck a camp in Baden-Württemberg as temperatures soared around the country. Several people were injured.

entry.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
entry.content: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

entry.title: Polish president strips Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honour
entry.content: Polish President Karol Nawrocki’s decision to strip Volodymyr Zelenskyy of the Order of the White Eagle has sparked controversy in Ukraine.

entry.title: Forget 'Do Not Touch': Choreographer William Forsythe invites you to dance with the exhibits
entry.content: The groundbreaking choreographer William Forsythe invites the public to visit his new exhibition, taking place in the Netherlands. There's a twist, as traditional museum rules don't apply.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 20th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 20th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Italian tourist dies in Dominican Republic resort blaze
entry.content: Around 1,690 tourists were evacuated from a luxury resort in the village of Bayahibe after a fire broke out.

entry.title: France swelters in heatwave as temperatures look set to hit 40C
entry.content: Nearly two-thirds of France is now under an orange heatwave alert as people battle extreme temperatures across the country.

entry.title: Andros AI, heritage and tourism: the technology transforming the visitor experience in Greece
entry.content: Andros AI is the first AI tour guide created for a Greek destination. It is essentially a chat service where visitors can ask anything about the island.

entry.title: French presidential frontrunner Jordan Bardella vows to win 2027 polls and shift course in the EU
entry.content: Jordan Bardella, leader of France's far-right National Rally, said he would like to "put the Commission and the EU back at the service of the nations and no longer the other way around".

entry.title: USA sail through to World Cup knockout stages as Scotland falter against Morocco
entry.content: Mauricio Pochettino's side are now guaranteed to finish top of Group D. They beat Paraguay 4-1 in their opening fixture.

entry.title: 'It's a lifestyle!': Museum of Youth Culture pays tribute to the bold beauty of British subcultures
entry.content: A celebration of the magic and messiness of misspent youth, this museum is for every teenager that once scrawled RAWR on their wrist, wore their eyeliner too thick, and refused to accept the world as it was.

entry.title: Lebanon says Israel has carried out a wave of fresh strikes, killing at least five people
entry.content: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

entry.title: Driver dies and 33 seriously injured after two trains collide in the UK
entry.content: More than 20 ambulances, six air ambulances and specialist hazardous area response teams attended the scene in southeast England.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 20th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 20th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Tired of jet lag? Experts share their top tips for travel recovery
entry.content: Jet lag can derail any trip, but experts say careful planning before, during and after a flight can help travellers adjust more quickly.

entry.title: Jordan Bardella meets Karol Nawrocki in Warsaw
entry.content: French far‑right leader Jordan Bardella on Thursday began a two‑day visit to Poland, seeking meetings with right‑wing and nationalist figures ahead of next year’s French presidential election, after similar trips to Portugal, Italy and Belgium.

entry.title: Sánchez backs international dialogue against other leaders: 'A Europe open to the world'
entry.content: Spain’s prime minister used the European Council summit to urge a more ambitious multiannual budget, and voiced solidarity with Meloni after Trump said she begged him for a photo.

entry.title: 'Not our Europe': Macron and Sánchez slam 'ineffective' return hubs for migrants
entry.content: "I don't know if these are the fundamental principles on which our Europe was built," Emmanuel Macron said as he strongly criticized the increasingly popular project to build return hubs for rejected asylum seekers.

entry.title: Heatwave nears 40C in France, forcing tourists to change routines
entry.content: France is enduring a widespread heatwave, with temperatures nearing 40C in parts of the country and forcing tourists and residents to change their routines.

entry.title: Judo: Japan dominates Day one in Ulaanbaatar
entry.content: Japan claimed four gold medals on the opening day of the Ulaanbaatar Grand Slam 2026. ABE Uta made history with a record-breaking 12th Grand Slam title, while KOGA Wakana, TAKEOKA Takeshi and OMORI Akari also struck gold. AGHAYEV Balabay defeated world champion NAGAYAMA Ryuju for -60kg gold.

entry.title: As Europe bakes, Berlin Zoo’s elephants and bears cool off
entry.content: Berlin Zoo uses “ice bombs” and water showers to help bears and elephants cool down as Europe faces an intense heatwave this weekend.

entry.title: EU Commission to develop diversification instrument, von der Leyen says
entry.content: The Commission is set to develop new trade defence tools, including a diversification instrument, to tackle the EU's "unsustainable" trade deficit with China, with the political backing of EU leaders.

entry.title: At the Web3 Summit, a growing movement wants to reclaim the internet for the people
entry.content: At the Web3 Summit in Berlin, developers, economists and activists debate whether decentralised technology can deliver on its promise to put control of the internet back into the hands of users.

entry.title: Airbus at Vivatech: How quantum sensing and AI can transform flying
entry.content: Airbus is testing radar, lidar, cameras and AI to help aircraft detect obstacles and give pilots better situational awareness. Euronews Next spoke to the head of their Optimate demonstrator project.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 19th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 19th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Norwegian parliament performs viral Viking row to back World Cup team
entry.content: Norway’s parliamentarians performed the viral "Viking row" inside the chamber on Thursday to back their national football team at the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew ceasefire, officials say
entry.content: Lebanese media reported an Israeli airstrike in Southern Lebanon on Friday, after a US official said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed on a ceasefire.

entry.title: EU Commission approves Hungary's revised €10bn recovery plan at Magyar's first EU summit
entry.content: The Commission has given rapid approval to the document submitted by the new government, which could be the key to unlocking €10 billion in recovery funds.

entry.title: EU leaders aim for October draft deal on €2 trillion budget
entry.content: Battle lines are being drawn between member states on EU spending, with southern and eastern states seeking more for agriculture and regional funding while hawkish governments push back.

entry.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The returning MP set to challenge Keir Starmer
entry.content: Burnham has said he was first inspired to enter politics after watching "Boys From The Blackstuff," an acclaimed 1982 TV show about five unemployed men navigating Margaret Thatcher's Britain.

entry.title: ‘We need to talk about this,’ Kallas-Israel spat draws mixed reactions from EU leaders
entry.content: EU's foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas faced a mixed reaction at the Brussels summit over alleged remarks comparing Israel to apartheid South Africa, after Israel cut contact with her.

entry.title: Would you pay €50 to visit Venice? New mayor is pushing for entry fee hike
entry.content: The council is also evaluating whether to extend the fee to the entire year, or perhaps include more dates.

entry.title: Time to bring back shame? Olivia Rodrigo reveals she has smelled diaper-wearing fans at live shows
entry.content: At a time when concert etiquette feels like it couldn't get much worse, the 23-year-old singer may have uncovered an entirely new frontier of diabolically shameful fan behaviour.

entry.title: Is it now safe to travel to the Middle East? What to know as UK downgrades advisories
entry.content: The new advice makes it easier for British holidaymakers to travel to destinations like Dubai and Doha.

entry.title: VivaTech 2026: Why learn German when AI can talk for you, asks DeepL CEO
entry.content: DeepL's CEO believes AI can erase the language barrier in business interactions entirely — but says learning a language is still worth it, because behind every language is a culture AI cannot translate.

entry.title: Matisse and Yves Saint Laurent come together in "Beauty, Fashion and Happiness" exhibition
entry.content: The exhibition held at the Musée Matisse Nice in France showcases 160 works by famous painter Henri Matisse and fashion designer Yves Saint Laurent. The exhibition runs until 28 September 2026.

entry.title: Game changer sweets: the Bavarian start-up making sustainable ‘chocolate’ from sunflowers
entry.content: Do you have a sweet tooth? Let’s dig into the world’s first cocoa-free chocolate alternative made from sunflower seeds. ‘Made in Europe’ learns the secret behind ChoViva, a Bavarian newcomer in the food industry.

entry.title: Von der Leyen told EU leaders it's right time to consider a mandate for talks with Russia
entry.content: European Commission President von der Leyen told EU leaders it is time to be prepared and consider an EU mandate to negotiate with Russia on Ukraine, as Costa's controversial Kremlin outreach draws pushback.

entry.title: Giorgia Meloni fires back at Trump's 'completely made-up claims'
entry.content: The Italian PM said on Friday that she was "shocked" at Trump's comments. The once close pair's relationship has been increasingly strained in recent months.

entry.title: Capgemini AI chief: Humans and AI don’t have the right chemistry — yet
entry.content: The innovation chief for the French IT consulting firm told Euronews Next why trust, regulation and a “plan B” for AI models will shape the next phase of AI adoption.

entry.title: Robin Hood’s famed Sherwood Forest 1,200-year-old oak tree declared dead
entry.content: It’s not the first time the legendary oak has been declared dead. On previous occasions, fears were raised only for the tree to stubbornly leaf out again each spring. Not this year.

entry.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
entry.content: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

entry.title: Estonia creates AI ‘ID Codes’ to govern autonomous agents
entry.content: The government says AI ID codes will make it easier for companies and individuals to automate work without giving an agent access to all their data.

entry.title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz amid reports Iran has reclosed the waterway
entry.content: The Persian Gulf Strait Authority has announced that ships that submit "compliant transit requests" would be allowed to transit the Strait.

entry.title: Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as Argentina captain’s father faces health issues
entry.content: The family of Argentina national football team captain Lionel Messi has issued a public statement confirming that his father, Jorge Messi, “is going through a health situation,” amid rumors about Jorge Messi's health. The family asked for “responsibility, prudence and humanity.”

entry.title: Cypriot minister vows to keep 'building' on EU accession momentum
entry.content: As the last days of Cyprus’s rotating EU presidency approach, Deputy Minister for European Affairs, Marilena Raouna, says her country will work "very hard" to open the remaining phases of talks on Ukraine and Moldova's EU membership.

entry.title: US-Iran deal is 'very fragile' as conflict 'remains', says analyst
entry.content: Tomi Huhtanen, Executive Director of the Wilfried Martens Centre, believes the EU should be "involved" in the peace deal. Huhtanen said the bloc needed to be "in the same group chat" when discussing potential contacts with Russia, arguing that coordination among European countries "has not worked".

entry.title: Who is the most evil Disney villain? The answer may surprise you
entry.content: The House of Mouse has given audiences some cracking scoundrels over the years. Some heinous, some nightmarish, and some deeply misunderstood. And one is especially irredeemable...

entry.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
entry.content: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

entry.title: From Pompeii to Évora : invisible solar for heritage sites
entry.content: Solar panels disguised as ancient Roman tiles or designed to blend into historic skylines. Pompeii and Évora are proving that heritage preservation and sustainability can go hand in hand.

entry.title: Iranian artist Parastoo Ahmadi reportedly sentenced to 74 lashes for singing without hijab
entry.content: Iranian singer Parastoo Ahmadi has been reportedly sentenced to 74 lashes for singing without hijab. For Iranian artists, the ruling deepens fears of escalating cultural repression.

entry.title: Uzbekistan celebrates first-ever World Cup goal despite defeat to Colombia
entry.content: The Central Asian country's World Cup debut brought early-morning crowds, large public screenings and a memorable goal that many supporters considered more important than the final score.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 19th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 19th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: The screams through the wall: the hidden toll of Russia's war against Ukraine
entry.content: On the International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict, Jacqueline Mahon, UNFPA Representative to Ukraine, writes in a Euronews op-ed that wartime sexual violence scars survivors, families and communities, while funding cuts threaten support services.

entry.title: Obama Presidential Centre opens with star-studded ceremony and veiled jabs at Trump
entry.content: On stage, Obama voiced his support for character, honesty, integrity, kindness, compassion and sense of duty, praising both Democrats and Republicans.

entry.title: Russian drones hit two foreign-flagged civilian ships in the Black Sea, Ukraine says
entry.content: Local air defences shot down 79 of 90 Russian drones from Thursday evening into Friday morning, per Ukraine's air force.

entry.title: Football not covered by US-Iran framework deal, Iran's World Cup squad finds
entry.content: Just two days after Washington and Tehran signed a framework deal aimed at ending their war, Iran's football federation said US travel restrictions on its World Cup squad remain unchanged, and is filing a formal complaint with FIFA.

entry.title: Europe looks at banning social media for teens, and girls are most impacted
entry.content: As the UK joins a growing list of countries looking at banning or severely curbing children's access to social media, data shows girls have higher levels of problematic social media use than boys across Europe.

entry.title: Farms, schools, rail and health services under strain as heatwave grips France
entry.content: With temperatures set to soar as high as 40°C in the coming days, many workers are already changing their routines to adapt to the imminent challenges. In Bordeaux’s vineyards, the working day now starts before dawn as to protect workers from peak climates during the middle of the day.

entry.title: Podcast | Ten years on, Brexit ghosts still linger
entry.content: Ten years ago, the UK voted to leave the European Union. On the eve of the 23 June anniversary, we look at how both the EU and the UK have changed since Brexit.

entry.title: 'All of Lebanon must burn,' Israeli minister says after IDF reports four soldiers killed
entry.content: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

entry.title: A cosmic tribute: Asteroid named after beloved singer Elliott Smith
entry.content: More than two decades after his death, Elliott Smith has been immortalized with an asteroid named after him. The celestial honour came from a fan listening to his song ‘Shooting Star’...

entry.title: Andy Burnham wins key UK by-election, setting up Labour leadership battle with Starmer
entry.content: In his victory speech, Burnham warned the Labour Party that it had a "final chance to change".

entry.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
entry.content: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

entry.title: Battle of the branding: Who is competing for inaugural European CMO of the Year at Cannes Lions
entry.content: From L'Oréal and Ferrari, to LEGO and Gymshark, 29 senior marketing leaders are competing for the inaugural European Chief Marketing Officer of the Year award at Cannes Lions.

entry.title: Newsletter: EU leaders poised for battle over cash
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: EU leaders react to European Council President António Costa laying the groundwork for diplomatic contacts with Russia; and exclusive comments from Sweden's EU affairs minister as EU leaders gear up to debate the bloc's next long-term budget.

entry.title: EU leaders kick off heated talks on €2 trillion long-term budget plan
entry.content: The EU's 27 leaders have kicked off their second day of talks in Brussels. On the table, the divisive issue of the next long-term budget, which is splitting EU governments into two opposing camps.

entry.title: JD Vance cancels trip to Switzerland for planned US-Iran talks
entry.content: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an initial agreement aimed at bringing the Iran war to a close on Wednesday.

entry.title: EU leaders debate Ukraine as Costa opens diplomatic channel with Kremlin
entry.content: EU leaders wrapped up talks in Brussels after President António Costa opened a diplomatic channel with the Kremlin. Volodymyr Zelenskyy renewed his call for fast-track EU membership, while Hungary's new Prime Minister Péter Magyar made his European Council debut.

entry.title: Mexico secures ticket to knockout stages as Canada breeze past Qatar with ruthless 6-0 win
entry.content: Mexico became the first team to secure a knockout rounds ticket after beating South Korea 1-0 to top Group A with six points on matchday two of the group stages.

entry.title: W thinks it has the X Factor: Europe’s alternative to mainstream social media
entry.content: The Swedish-based “W” platform” is the latest in a series of new social media sites vying to replace US Big Tech companies.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 19th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 19th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: EU leaders to hold talks on common budget at Brussels summit as battle for cash begins
entry.content: EU governments will hold substantial negotiations on how to allocate €2 trillion of the European common budget over the next seven years, pitting frugals calling for cuts against member states defending money for agriculture to be preserved in tough talks ahead.

entry.title: From oak forests to steppe lakes: Central Europe's five best summer destinations
entry.content: Bored of the crowded seasides, the heat island big cities? Visit Central Europe's lakes, zoos and other exciting places that can unite all generations.

entry.title: Timelapse video shows ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz since reopening
entry.content: A timelapse shows vessels moving through the Strait of Hormuz after the US and Iran signed an agreement ending their conflict and reopening one of the world's most important shipping routes.

entry.title: Hegseth announces review of US forces in Europe as he lambasts NATO allies in Brussels meeting
entry.content: Hegseth slammed Europe for prioritising what he called liberal ideals over practical defence needs as he lambasted the alliance’s defence chiefs in a rare appearance at the NATO HQ in Brussels.

entry.title: Ukraine deserves fast EU membership ‘more than any other country,’ Zelenskyy says
entry.content: Zelenskyy called on EU leaders to open all accession clusters in the coming weeks and eventually endorse Kyiv’s fast-track membership bid, saying it "deserves full EU membership more than any other European country”.

entry.title: Costa defends surprise decision to open diplomatic channel with Russia
entry.content: António Costa’s surprise decision to open a diplomatic channel with Russia dominated discussions at Thursday’s summit, as several leaders expressed concern that they had not been consulted before the call was launched.

entry.title: An EU of six, not 27, best way to 'reinforce Europe', Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews
entry.content: The former French finance minister argued that new impetus should be given to the European Union, namely by uniting six core countries to deliver on key issues in the face of foreign threats.

entry.title: Péter Magyar's EU summit debut signals Hungarian reset after change of guard
entry.content: Magyar was met with optimism at his first EU summit in Brussels, while Orbán rallied far-right allies nearby at much smaller venue urging him to veto the bloc's next seven-year budget. For the institutions, the Orbán era is over.

entry.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
entry.content: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

entry.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets
entry.content: New York erupted in jubilant scenes on Saturday when the Knicks broke a 53-year drought to defeat the San Antonio Spurs away in Texas.

entry.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
entry.content: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

entry.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
entry.content: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

entry.title: UK police say man under arrest after toddler 'ended up' in crocodile pen at zoo
entry.content: Cambridgeshire Constabulary said the toddler had sustained "serious injuries" and was in a "critical but stable" condition at Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge.

entry.title: Foreign aid cuts and climate change pushing up migrant flows, IOM chief warns
entry.content: Several rich Western countries, particularly the United States but also many European nations, have cut their development aid budgets in recent years.

entry.title: Companies are rushing into AI, but adoption is still lagging, a KPMG executive says
entry.content: Companies may have AI strategies, but few can prove they are paying off, according to KPMG’s recent report. Euronews Next spoke with Mathieu Wallich-Petit, Head of Clients & Markets at KPMG France about how businesses can move from AI pilots to real impact.

entry.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
entry.content: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
entry.content: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

entry.title: French fishermen gather in Cherbourg as sector faces down multiple challenges
entry.content: In total, the French government said €13 million would be dedicated to the fishing sector for fuel consumption in April and May.

entry.title: Fact check: Can EU countries restrict or ban Israeli settlement imports?
entry.content: Several EU states are weighing measures against goods from Israeli settlements in the West Bank, East Jerusalem and the Golan Heights – all considered illegal under international law. Some argue that rules that exclude such goods from Israel’s tariff-free trade deal are not being properly enforced.

entry.title: Switzerland heading towards referendum on construction of new nuclear plants
entry.content: During the debates in parliament, Energy Minister Albert Rosti stressed the need to keep the nuclear power option open to guarantee the country's long-term energy supply.

entry.title: Bruce Springsteen, Stevie Wonder and Bono to attend Obama Centre's star-studded opening
entry.content: The $850 million campus, located on Chicago's South Side, includes a museum, library, sports facilities and community spaces.

entry.title: Keeping up appearances: Greece reveals Parthenon façade after 220 years
entry.content: For the first time in about 220 years, the west façade of the Parthenon in Greece can be see in its fullest possible form. External scaffolding has now been permanently removed from the monument, marking a key step in its long restoration.

entry.title: The energy transition's next test is security
entry.content: 'Countries that emerge strongest from the next phase of the transition will be those with diversified energy systems that remain secure, affordable and sustainable in a more uncertain world' writes Roberto Bocca from World Economic Forum’s Centre for Energy and Materials in an OpEd for Euronews.

entry.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
entry.content: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

entry.title: New Lithuanian coalition formed as Social Democrats strike deal with partners
entry.content: Lithuania’s Social Democrats have formed a third coalition in just one and a half years, clearing the way for a new cabinet to be led by party leader Mindaugas Sinkevičius.

entry.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
entry.content: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

entry.title: EU summit: Leaders and Zelenskyy to debate accession and peace negotiations
entry.content: Russia's war on Ukraine and unfair Chinese competition are expected to dominate the first day of the EU summit in Brussels.

entry.title: Chinese-British nationals jailed after being convicted of spying on exiled Hong Kong dissidents
entry.content: The sweeping National Security Law, which severely curtailed freedoms in Hong Kong, contributed to years of strained ties between Beijing and London.

entry.title: Palestine Football Association denies reported FIFA youth match against Israel
entry.content: The PFA said it had "not received any official communication or proposal from FIFA regarding such a match".

entry.title: Bulgarian prime minister opposes EU sanctions on Russia's Patriarch Kirill
entry.content: "The era of the Crusades is over," the Bulgarian prime minister said on Thursday as he confirmed his opposition to the proposal.

entry.title: Middle East flights update: Which airlines are returning to the region and when?
entry.content: While the US and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding, European airlines are unlikely to restart flights until after the summer.

entry.title: Rutte tries to downplay Pete Hegseth's blistering attack on NATO
entry.content: As the Trump admininstration plans to pull a number troops out of Europe in the next six months, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth lambasted NATO defence ministers in Brussels, describing them as "shameful" and preoccupied with gender equality and climate change.

entry.title: Scotland for the win: Massachusetts becomes first US state to 'legalise' haggis
entry.content: The Scottish staple has been illegal in the US since the 1970s. Following a pre-World Cup campaign by one of Scotland's leading butchers and the efforts of a Scottish podcaster, Massachusetts has become the first US state to finally legalise haggis. Officially. Ish.

entry.title: Graft probe into Spanish ex-PM Zapatero expanded to daughters and secretary, court says
entry.content: The former premier defended his innocence during a three-hour hearing before Calama on Wednesday, becoming the first former or serving Spanish head of government to declare as a suspect in a corruption investigation.

entry.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
entry.content: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

entry.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama in Berlin: Europe today needs courage for enlargement
entry.content: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment? Albania's PM Edi Rama makes his case in Berlin

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Toy Story 5' - You've got a friend in tech?
entry.content: The toys face a new existential crisis: technology. As timely as it is, can the fifth instalment recapture the magic lacking in 'Toy Story 4'?

entry.title: Poland arrests suspect in killing of Russian artist critical of Putin, PM Donald Tusk says
entry.content: Since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has been accused of trying to assassinate its opponents abroad, including targeting exiled activists in France and Lithuania.

entry.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
entry.content: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

entry.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
entry.content: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

entry.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old 'proto-Stonehenge' just a few miles from the famous monument
entry.content: The structure consisted of two wooden posts that aligned precisely with the summer sunrise and winter sunset, suggesting ancient Britons were already tracking the Sun before the creation of Stonehenge.

entry.title: Salesforce France CEO: Both leaders and employees need to adapt to AI
entry.content: Speaking at Vivatech in Paris, Emilie Sidiqian, Salesforce France’s CEO, told Euronews Next how companies should embrace AI and why leaders must drive its adoption from the top.

entry.title: From Foxconn to Nvidia: Why France is so attractive for Europe’s AI infrastructure
entry.content: Foxconn, Nvidia and Mistral AI announce major AI infrastructure deals at Europe's VivaTech conference, with France's cheap nuclear energy and homegrown talent drawing global investment.

entry.title: EU-backed green bonds risk financing Chinese clean tech in third countries
entry.content: The EU has launched a €15-20bn green investment fund to boost sustainable projects, but a good share of the money is set to go towards buying Chinese clean technologies – including the same high-risk power inverters Brussels is trying to phase out.

entry.title: Joel and Ethan Coen named as this year’s Lumière Award recipients
entry.content: The 18th Lumière Awards will honour not one but two giants of the silver screen...

entry.title: England’s captain fantastic: Harry Kane praised for a ‘complete performance’ against Croatia
entry.content: The Bayern Munich striker scored twice as the Three Lions beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup group L opening fixture.

entry.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
entry.content: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

entry.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
entry.content: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

entry.title: World's longest non-stop flight set for 2027 take-off as Qantas confirms Sydney-London route
entry.content: Qantas’s long-awaited ‘Project Sunrise’ will see the world's longest non-stop passenger flight cover more than 16,000 kilometres between 19 and 22 hours.

entry.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, so will Moscow,’ Zelenskyy says after refinery fire
entry.content: The Ukrainian president said on Thursday that Kyiv will respond to all Russian attacks and that the morning strike on Moscow’s refinery — in response to Russia's attack on a UNESCO-protected cathedral earlier this week was “entirely justified.”

entry.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
entry.content: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Besides Trump, nobody believes deal will last, former US general says
entry.content: Retired US General Ben Hodges believes the situation is now "much worse" than four months ago. With Israel and Lebanon left out of the deal between the US and Iran, Hodges thinks the potential for Israel and Hezbollah to "resume fighting is really high".

entry.title: Inside the ultra-luxe Swiss mountainside retreat where Iran and the US are set to meet
entry.content: Initial negotiations between the US and Iran on the final agreement are set to take place at the Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne this Friday.

entry.title: Voters head to the polls in key UK by-election that could determine Britain's next PM
entry.content: Burnham is regarded by many Labour MPs as the party's best chance of recovery following the disastrous May local elections.

entry.title: Misinformation targets Trump and European leaders at G7
entry.content: Social media users have been sharing old clips and taking statements by politicians out of context, in an apparent effort to highlight the strained relationship between the US and Europe.

entry.title: Cristiano Ronaldo faces media backlash as pundits slam Portugal's display against DR Congo
entry.content: The 41-year-old Portugal captain was hit by a barrage of criticism after a display that fell short of expectations at what is his sixth and, possibly, last World Cup.

entry.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
entry.content: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

entry.title: Shipping industry wary of resuming Strait of Hormuz navigation despite imminent deal
entry.content: The world's most important energy chokepoint may soon reopen, but insurance markets must regain confidence for the shipping sector to resume normal navigation. Meanwhile, Germany has dispatched two naval vessels for a possible mine clearance operation.

entry.title: Where is EV charging cheapest and fastest?
entry.content: Europe's EV charging network is growing fast, but prices and refuelling point density vary widely across the continent. Europe in Motion breaks down the data.

entry.title: VivaTech 2026: Tips from L'Oréal and PwC on personal vitality and business resilience
entry.content: From skincare giant L'Oréal to consulting firm PwC, business leaders at VivaTech 2026 had a shared message: personal vitality and organisational resilience are no longer a luxury — they are a competitive necessity.

entry.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
entry.content: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

entry.title: Hegseth says Europe must take the lead on building 'NATO 3.0'
entry.content: US president Donald Trump has repeatedly pushed for European NATO allies to divert more money to defence spending.

entry.title: 'The Ring' and 'Lilo & Stitch' actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
entry.content: Horror movie fans will recognize Chase from her performance as Samara Morgan, the main antagonist in American remake of 'The Ring'.
